channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: AI and Its Discontents
item.description: With AI hype reaching fever pitch, a backlash has begun, with university graduates, in particular, resisting a technology that could quickly render their skills obsolete. They are not alone in challenging AI boosters’ bubbly narrative. While the technology will transform the economy and upend labor markets, important bottlenecks and major risks will limit its productivity benefits—and the payoff for investors.

item.title: The Big Picture
Trump Is Treating Taiwan Like Collateral
item.description: The pause of a $14 billion arms package raises concerns about U.S. support for Taipei.

item.title: Centrists crying “Wolf!”
item.description: The wrong way to beat populists who deny the existence of hard problems

item.title: Ukraine’s latest challenge is how to deal with hope
item.description: Besides beating Russia, Ukraine must fight corruption and build democracy

item.title: U.S. Launches ‘Self-Defense’ Strikes on Iran
item.description: Still, Washington insists that it is committed to peace talks with Tehran.

item.title: The Iran War Has Remade the Gulf
item.description: The region knows that Iran won the war—and is hedging its bets as a result.

item.title: Giga-IPOs are a symptom of public markets’ giga-problem
item.description: The incredible shrinking stockmarket

item.title: The Trump administration’s big move to limit legal immigration
item.description: A new policy restricting green-card applications could wreak havoc

item.title: Xi Ascendant
item.description: Trump’s Beijing trip shows that the wind is blowing in China’s direction.

item.title: Why the U.S. Should Wind Down Military Aid to Israel
item.description: Washington should no longer be liable for Israeli misdeeds.

item.title: China’s New Type 054B Frigate Joins Liaoning Carrier Group for First Time in Western Pacific
item.description: Japan confirmed the first known deployment of a Jiangkai III-class frigate as part of a PLAN carrier strike group, highlighting China’s accelerating blue-water naval integration.

item.title: The Gangster Logic of Trump’s Tariff Wars
item.description: US President Donald Trump has turned tariffs into instruments of extortion, pressuring trading partners into investment commitments that advance his political and economic interests. Once political favoritism supplants market competition as the organizing principle of global commerce, severe distortions are inevitable.

item.title: More Than Trade: Modi’s Visit to Sweden and Norway
item.description: Modi’s Nordic tour could also be interpreted as the opening move in a deliberate strategy to build India’s Arctic credentials. But there’s a big obstacle: Russia.

item.title: Theresa Sabonis-Helf on Central Asia’s Thirsty Future Industries
item.description: “I don’t find evidence that [Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan] are yet grappling with the water needs of next-generation industries.”

item.title: Can the Climate Crisis Unite Europe?
item.description: The foundations of Europe’s postwar peace and prosperity are beginning to fracture, yet the European Union still struggles to unite its increasingly divided member states around a common political project. The escalating climate crisis could provide the continent with the shared purpose it has long lacked.

item.title: Amid Rumors of a Visit by China’s Leader, North Korea Fires Close-Range Ballistic Missiles
item.description: North Korea intends to press ahead with its own military modernization timeline regardless of the diplomatic calendar.

item.title: China’s world-beating solar industry is in turmoil
item.description: The Gulf war won’t save it

item.title: Brussels Needs a New Narrative on China-EU Relations
item.description: If Europe wants to reduce security vulnerabilities in its economic relationship with China, it must embed those policies within a strategically positive narrative.

item.title: How Many AI Regulators Has the Pope?
item.description: Artificial intelligence may be humanity’s greatest opportunity and risk. Where moral leaders have too little influence and states cannot align, actors with system-wide exposure and cross-border reach—in other words, large asset owners—become the only entities capable of imposing needed constraints at scale.

item.title: China Wants to Speak for Taiwan. The World Should Hear Taiwan Directly.
item.description: China wants the world to hear only one version of Taiwan’s story: Beijing’s. And much of the world is listening to this version.

item.title: Will Australia’s Independents Form a Political Party?
item.description: New campaign finance laws put the Community Independents Project at a crossroads.

item.title: Pakistan and the Economics of Diplomacy
item.description: Khurram Husain joins host Tushar Shetty to examine whether Pakistan’s recent diplomatic gains are translating into meaningful economic progress.

item.title: How China Got One of the World’s Largest Human Rights Convenings Canceled
item.description: RightsCon 2026 was supposed to be held in Zambia in May – but then Beijing pressured the host government.

item.title: China’s Quiet Pivot to Central Asian Gas
item.description: Putin left Beijing last week without a Power of Siberia 2 deal. The reason is partly in Turkmenistan.

item.title: No, the Quad Isn’t Dead. This Week’s Foreign Ministers’ Meeting Proves It.
item.description: Public-facing work, such as leaders’ meetings, is necessary, but even without it the Quad provides a vital mechanism for its members to collectively counter China’s behavior.

item.title: There Is More Than One Cockroach in Narendra Modi’s Kitchen
item.description: The emergence of the Cockroach Janta Party indicates that discontent within and with the Modi government may run far deeper than it appears.

item.title: Taking Women Farmers Seriously
item.description: Rather than designing solutions for half the farming population, agricultural researchers, policymakers, and others must consider the specific needs and preferences of women farmers in developing countries. Only then can the huge female agricultural labor force reach its potential to ensure food security and drive economic growth.

item.title: Cover Story newsletter: A Starship enterprise
item.description: How we chose this week’s images

item.title: Growing Sino-Russian Cooperation Worries India
item.description: At the Beijing summit, Putin and Xi oversaw the signing of 20 pacts. But a much-anticipated deal on the Power of Siberia-1 pipeline did not materialize.

item.title: American Decline on Display
item.description: Donald Trump’s state visit to Beijing has further demonstrated the relative weakness of the United States and the West vis-à-vis the People’s Republic of China and the Global South. While Chinese President Xi Jinping is surely happy with the outcome, Europe now has even more reasons to worry about its future.

item.title: The G-2 Reality
item.description: America and China cannot dominate or exclude each other.

item.title: The War in Ethiopia Isn’t Over
item.description: Peace deals are leading to more violence.

item.title: Cambodia to Mark May 28 as the Beginning of its Border War With Thailand
item.description: The country's ruling party is using an unnecessary conflict as a chance to build up its political myth.

item.title: Hun Sen Feels the Heat
item.description: The Cambodian autocrat’s anti-scam rhetoric and pardoning of Kem Sokha suggest that outside pressure is starting to work. That's all the more reason to intensify it.

item.title: Indonesia Bans Polymarket After Site Offers Bets on President’s Ouster
item.description: The country has become the second in Southeast Asia to block access to the controversial prediction market.

item.title: Former Cambodian Opposition Leader Kem Sokha Granted Royal Pardon
item.description: The move comes as the country's government faces growing scrutiny for its role in handling an explosion of online scam centers and organized crime.

item.title: A High Court in India Recognizes Yet Another Disputed Site as a Hindu Temple
item.description: The Bhojshala complex in Madhya Pradesh houses a temple dedicated to the Hindu goddess Saraswati, and also a mosque associated with a medieval-era Sufi saint.

item.title: Europe Is Slowly Getting Ready to Ditch America
item.description: Trump’s bad bargains have shaken a complacent continent.

item.title: Attacks on Sufis and Their Shrines are Growing in Bangladesh
item.description: The Jamaat-e-Islami and affiliated groups are attacking Bangladesh’s long tradition of cultural and social pluralism.
